title: cistus × pulverulentus f. delilei
intro: Cistus × pulverulentus f. delilei, also known as the Delile's rock rose or the gum rock rose, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Cistus genus, which is a type of shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The gum rock rose is also valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting the body from oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Cistus × pulverulentus f. delilei can be taken in the form of tea, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
